one_time_description: Write down of tangible capital assets
economic_notes: Nation operates marina, golf course, accommodations, restaurant, VLT, property tax and lease income from lot leases. Buffalo Point Development Corp. Ltd. and 5426831 Manitoba Ltd. consolidated. Twenty-unit and two-unit and three-unit rental housing projects under CMHC. Lot leases range from 1-69 years. GST revenue sharing agreement with federal government. Buffalo Point First Nation sales tax and income tax collected.
notes: Nation is in Manitoba (not AB). Four entities consolidated: Buffalo Point First Nation Housing Authority, Buffalo Point Development Corp. Ltd., Buffalo Point First Nation, and 5426831 Manitoba Ltd. Net debt position of -2,169,473. Large deferred revenue balance of $12.5M primarily from lot leases (1-69 year terms) and ISC. Promissory note of $198,623 payable to Receiver General for Canada is interest-free and was due March 31, 2024 — ISC indicated renewal will be needed. Contaminated sites exist but no liability recorded. CMHC replacement reserve underfunded by $43,014. Salaries figure taken from cash flow statement (cash paid to employees). No remuneration schedule found in provided text. OSR calculated as total revenue minus ISC ($5,510,855) and CMHC ($108,255) and First Peoples Development Inc. ($24,049) transfers = $6,521,403 net of deferred revenue adjustments; total government transfers include ISC + ISC Health + CMHC + First Peoples Development Inc.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
